title: Systems and methods of ultrasonic sensing in smart devices
abstract: The various embodiments described herein include methods, devices, and systems for ultrasonic sensing on electronic devices. In one aspect, a method is performed at an electronic device having memory, one or more processors, a speaker, and a microphone. The method includes, while audibly communicating with a user via the speaker and microphone: (1) sending one or more ultrasound pulses via the speaker; (2) receiving, via the microphone, one or more signals corresponding to the one or more ultrasound pulses; (3) determining positioning of the user based on the one or more received signals; and (4) adjusting one or more parameters of the speaker and/or the microphone based on the determined positioning.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: at an electronic device having memory, one or more processors, a speaker, and a microphone: while audibly communicating with a user via the speaker and microphone: sending one or more ultrasound pulses via the speaker; receiving, via the microphone, one or more signals corresponding to the one or more ultrasound pulses; and determining positioning of the user based on the one or more received signals. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising: scanning ultrasound environs of the electronic device; determining, based on the scanning, one or more ultrasound parameters for the one or more ultrasound pulses; wherein the one or more ultrasound pulses are sent with the one or more ultrasound parameters. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the one or more ultrasound parameters includes one or more frequency settings and/or one or more timing settings. 4. The method of claim 2 or 3, wherein scanning the ultrasound environs includes sending one or more test pulses. 5. The method of any of claims 2-4, wherein scanning the ultrasound environs comprises determining a signal to noise ratio for each of a plurality of frequencies. 6. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising identifying the user based on the one or more received signals. 7. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: identifying one or more user gestures based on the one or more received signals; and generating a response to the user based on the one or more user gestures. 8. The method of any preceding claim, wherein determining the positioning of the user comprises determining a distance between the user and the electronic device. 9. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: identifying one or more breathing cues of the user based on the one or more received signals; and generating a response to the user based on the one or more breathing cues. 10. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: emitting one or more second ultrasound pulses configured to determine a temperature of environs of the electronic device; receiving one or more second signals corresponding to the second ultrasound pulses; and determining the temperature of the environs based on the one or more second signals. 11. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising adjusting one or more parameters of the speaker and/or microphone based on the determined positioning of the user. 12. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising identifying movement of the user based on the one or more received signals. 13. The method of any preceding claim, wherein each ultrasound pulse of the one or more ultrasound pulses comprises a chirp sweeping across multiple frequencies. 14. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: determining whether a person is in a vicinity of the electronic device; and in accordance with a determination that a person is in the vicinity of the electronic device, greeting the person. 15. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: detecting motion via a sensor of the electronic device; in response to detecting the motion, sending one or more second ultrasound pulses; receiving one or more second signals corresponding to the one or more second ultrasound pulses; and characterizing the motion based on the one or more second signals. 16. The method of claim 15, further comprising enabling a virtual assistant feature based on the characterized motion. 17. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising emitting one or more ultrasound pulses configured to discourage a pet from approaching the electronic device. 18. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising adjusting a volume level of the speaker in accordance with the determined positioning of the user. 19. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising receiving positioning data from one or more second electronic devices; wherein determining the positioning of the user is further based on the received positioning data. 20. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising differentiating between the user and one or more additional entities based on the one or more received signals. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the differentiating is based on respiratory patterns of the user. 22. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: sending one or more radar pulses via a transmitter at the electronic device; receiving, via a receiver at the electronic device, one or more second signals corresponding to the one or more radar pulses; and wherein determining the positioning of the user is further based on the one or more second signals. 23. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: receiving one or more wireless communication signals; and wherein determining the positioning of the user is further based on the one or more wireless communication signals. 24. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: capturing, via an image sensor of the electronic device, one or more images of a scene in a field of view of the electronic device; and wherein determining the positioning of the user is further based on analysis of the one or more images of the scene. 25. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising notifying a second device in proximity of the user of the presence of the user based on the determined positioning of the user. 26. A method, comprising: at an electronic device having memory, one or more processors, a speaker, and a microphone: sending a first set of ultrasound chirps at a first rate via the speaker; receiving, via the microphone, a first set of signals corresponding to the first set of ultrasound chirps; determining based on the first set of signals that a person is in proximity to the electronic device; and in accordance with the determination that the person is in proximity to the electronic device, sending a second set of ultrasound chirps at a second rate, faster than the first rate. 27. The method of claim 26, further comprising: receiving, via the microphone, a second set of signals corresponding to the second set of ultrasound chirps; and identifying a gesture from the person based on the second set of signals. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the electronic device comprises at least one additional microphone; and wherein identifying the gesture from the person comprises comparing signals received by the at least one additional microphone with respective signals of the second set of signals. 29. The method of claim 26 or claim 27, further comprising: determining a relative distance to the person; and adjusting a chirp amplitude of the second set of ultrasound chirps based on the relative distance to the person. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the electronic device includes a user interface; and the method further comprises adjusting one or more characteristics of the user interface based on relative positioning of the person. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein adjusting the one or more characteristics of the user interface based on the relative positioning of the person comprises activating a display in accordance with the person being within a predetermined distance of the electronic device. 32. The method of claim 30 or 31, wherein adjusting the one or more characteristics of the user interface based on the relative positioning of the person comprises adjusting a font size of displayed text. 33. The method of any of claims 30-32, wherein adjusting the one or more characteristics of the user interface based on the relative positioning of the person comprises adjusting a volume level of audible content output by the electronic device. 34. The method of any of claims 26-33, further comprising: determining based on the second set of signals that the person is no longer in proximity to the electronic device; and in accordance with the determination that the person is no longer in proximity to the electronic device, sending a third set of ultrasound chirps at a third rate, slower than the second rate. 35. The method of any of claims 26-34, wherein determining that the person is in proximity to the electronic device comprises identifying proximity of the person based on temporal variations in the first set of signals. 36. The method of any of claims 26-35, wherein determining that the person is in proximity to the electronic device includes: identifying a segment of the first set of signals, the segment consistent with a person in proximity to the electronic device; and determining whether the segment meets one or more detection criteria. 37. The method of claim 36, further comprising: detecting a confounding circumstance; and in accordance with detecting the confounding circumstance, adjusting the one or more detection criteria. 38. The method of claim 37, wherein the confounding circumstance comprises a fan operating in proximity with the electronic device. 39. The method of claim 37, wherein the confounding circumstance comprises an appliance vibrating in proximity with the electronic device. 40. The method of any of claims 37-39, wherein adjusting the one or more detection criteria comprises increasing a proximity detection threshold. 41. The method of any of claims 37-39, wherein adjusting the one or more detection criteria comprises disabling proximity detection for a preset amount of time or until the confounding circumstance is no longer detected. 42. The method of any of claims 26-41, further comprising maintaining a mapping of signals of the first set of signals to respective ultrasound chirps of the first set of ultrasound chirps. 43. The method of claim 42, further comprising, identifying a room response from the mapping; and wherein determining that the person is in proximity to the electronic device comprises identifying a variance from the room response. 44. The method of claim 43, further comprising: obtaining environmental data for environs of the electronic device; and updating the identified room response based on the environmental data. 45. The method of any of claims 42-44, further comprising, in response to a disruption of the mapping, establishing a new mapping by increasing a chirp rate of subsequent ultrasound chirps for a preset amount of time. 46. The method of claim 45, further comprising, in response to the disruption of the mapping, discarding the mapping. 47. The method of claim 45 or 46, wherein the disruption is due to a buffer overflow at the electronic device. 48. An electronic device, comprising: one or more processors; a microphone; a speaker; and 

 memory storing one or more instructions that, when executed by the one or more processors, cause the electronic device to perform any of the methods of claims 1-47. 49. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium storing one or more programs, the one or more programs comprising instructions which, when executed by an electronic device with one or more processors, memory, a microphone, and a speaker, cause the electronic device to perform any of the methods of claims 1-47.
